entries.title: Big O Notation
entries.content: Describes how algorithm runtime or space grows with input size n. O(1) constant, O(log n) logarithmic, O(n) linear, O(n log n) linearithmic, O(n²) quadratic.

entries.title: Binary Search
entries.content: Find target in sorted array by halving search space each step. Very efficient: O(log n) time.

entries.title: REST API Principles
entries.content: Stateless HTTP API using resources and standard methods: GET (read), POST (create), PUT/PATCH (update), DELETE (remove).

entries.title: Machine Learning Basics
entries.content: Models learn patterns from data. Supervised: labeled examples. Unsupervised: find structure. Training minimizes a loss function.

entries.title: Git Version Control
entries.content: Track code changes with commits. Branches isolate features. Merge combines work. Never force-push shared main branches.

entries.title: SQL vs NoSQL
entries.content: SQL databases use structured tables and relationships (PostgreSQL, MySQL). NoSQL offers flexible documents or key-value stores (MongoDB, Redis).
